title: Dryopteris craspedosora
taxonomy: Dryopteris craspedosora is classified within the family Dryopteridaceae. This species belongs to the genus Dryopteris and is a type of fern. The classification is as follows: kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, family Dryopteridaceae, genus Dryopteris, species D. craspedosora. intro: Dryopteris craspedosora, also known as the shield fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The shield fern has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of the shield fern include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with the shield fern include teas and infusions, as well as extracts in capsule or tablet form, which are used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
